title: Passage 1
passage: In recent years, many organizations have adopted hybrid work models. Employees now divide their time between home and office environments. This flexibility often improves work-life balance and reduces commuting stress. However, it also introduces challenges such as communication gaps, inconsistent collaboration, and difficulty maintaining team cohesion. Companies that succeed with hybrid work usually establish clear communication protocols, regular check-ins, and measurable performance outcomes.

questions.question: What is one benefit of hybrid work mentioned in the passage?
Which challenge is associated with hybrid work?
The main idea of the passage is that hybrid work
options: is only useful for large companies

title: Passage 2
passage: Public transportation systems play a vital role in urban mobility. Efficient buses and metro services reduce traffic congestion and lower carbon emissions. Despite these advantages, many commuters still prefer private vehicles due to concerns about crowding and schedule reliability. To encourage wider adoption, city planners are investing in cleaner fleets, digital tracking applications, and integrated ticketing systems that simplify travel across multiple routes.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of public transport?
Why do some commuters avoid public transport?
What are city planners investing in?

title: Passage 3
passage: Reading habits among students have changed with the rise of digital media. While printed books remain valuable for deep concentration, digital platforms provide quick access to diverse resources. Educators suggest combining both formats: students can use digital tools for research and printed texts for focused study sessions. This blended approach can improve comprehension, reduce screen fatigue, and help learners adapt to different academic tasks.

What is a benefit of digital platforms?
A blended approach may help students
